content:
# Two Phase Titration

**Summary**: A method to determine surfactant concentration in a mixture of oil and water using a titrant like HTAB.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1987-ahmed|Ahmed, 1987]] used two-phase titration to measure residual surfactant concentrations in the presence and absence of crude oil.

- [[1987-fuseni|Fuseni, 1987]] used two-phase titration to determine the concentration of surfactants in the system.

## Instruments and Software



- [[1987-fuseni|Fuseni, 1987]] used two-phase titration to determine the concentration of surfactants in the system.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The technique was found to pose problems due to color interference from crude oil, requiring smaller aliquots for analysis. ([[1987-ahmed|Ahmed, 1987, p. 48]])

- The technique was limited by color interference from crude oil, requiring small sample volumes for accurate results. ([[1987-fuseni|Fuseni, 1987, p. 51]])
